perturbation.original_text: iii. Stock. The Company will issue Contractor 3,000,000 restricted shares of common stock upon execution of this Agreement as fully earned.
perturbation.changed_text: iii. Stock. The Company may consider issuing Contractor stock options, subject to board approval.
perturbation.explanation: The original text guarantees the issuance of 3,000,000 restricted shares. The changed text makes the stock options conditional and subject to board approval, introducing uncertainty where there was certainty before. This creates an in-text contradiction, because stock issuance that was defined as fully earned, is now subject to approval, which is contradictory
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor agrees to be compensated by the Company as follows: i. Fee The Company will pay Contractor a mutually agreed upon Fee for the first full year of service. ii. For the first year only a Payment of $25,000 of the Fee shall be advanced to Contractor within the first six months of this Agreement. Additionally, the Contractor will be paid a fee of $10,416.66 per month over the next 12 months. Allowing the contractor to be paid the above agreed upon amount of $150,000 for first twelve months iii. Stock. The Company may consider issuing Contractor stock options, subject to board approval. iv. The Company will reimburse Contractor for reasonable business expenses, including travel and temporary lodging, incurred on behalf of the Company. Any additional expenses shall be pre-approved by the Company and will be reimbursed subject to receiving reasonable substantiating documentation relating to such expenses.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the contractor would receive 3,000,000 restricted shares of common stock. The changed text states that the company 'may consider issuing Contractor stock options, subject to board approval.' This introduces uncertainty and conditionality (board approval) where there was a guarantee before. The Contradicted Text repeats the updated language, showing that this text is included in the full text of the agreement. This represents a strong contradiction because the original clause guaranteed stock, whereas the changed clause makes it subject to approval. Because the changed text is duplicated verbatim, there is a strong contradiction between the original issuance agreement and the altered issuance agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Contractor agrees that, upon request and without compensation therefore, but at no expense to Contractor, and whether during the time Contractor is working for the Company, or thereafter, Contractor will do all lawful acts, including the execution of papers and lawful oaths and the giving of testimony, that in the opinion of the Company, its successors and assigns, may be necessary or desirable in obtaining, sustaining, reissuing, extending and enforcing United States and foreign Letters Patent, including design patents, on all of such Company Inventions, and for perfecting, affirming, maintaining and recording the Company’s complete ownership and title thereto, and to otherwise cooperate in all proceedings and matters relating thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: Contractor agrees that, upon request, Contractor will cooperate with the Company to do all lawful acts necessary in obtaining patents, but the extent of cooperation will be subject to a separate agreement regarding compensation and expenses.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires the contractor to assist with patent-related activities 'without compensation'. The change introduces the need for a 'separate agreement regarding compensation,' which directly contradicts the original 'without compensation' clause. This creates an internal contradiction and uncertainty about the contractor's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor agrees that, upon request, Contractor will cooperate with the Company to do all lawful acts necessary in obtaining patents, but the extent of cooperation will be subject to a separate agreement regarding compensation and expenses. E. Exception . Contractor has listed below all unpatented, but potentially patentable, ideas and inventions conceived prior to our work for the Company (and which have not been assigned to a former employer) and which are, therefore, excluded from the scope of this Agreement: 1. __None Noted_______________________________________________; 2. _________________________________________________; 3. _________________________________________________; and 4. _________________________________________________.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the contractor will assist with patent activities 'without compensation'. The changed text introduces a requirement for a 'separate agreement regarding compensation and expenses'. This change directly contradicts the original 'without compensation' clause because the Changed Text now requires a seperate agreement for compensation, which contradicts the initial agreement of no compensation. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as the Contradicted Text cannot be adhered to, based on the explanation given, therefore the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: V. NO UNFAIR COMPETITION . As consideration for entering into this Agreement with the Company, and the Company providing Contractor compensation for our services, Contractor agrees not to compete with the Company as follows: A. During The Time Contractor is Working for the Company . Contractor agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not interfere with the business of the Company in any manner. Particularly, but without limitation, Contractor agrees to refrain from planning or organizing a competitive business during the time Contractor is working for the Company. Contractor further agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not have any proprietary interest in any competitive business except for an interest of less than five percent (5%) of the outstanding shares of a publicly-held corporation, meaning a corporation whose outstanding shares are owned by one hundred (100) or more shareholders. Contractor further agrees that during the time Contractor is working for the Company, Contractor will not solicit customers of the Company for any purpose whatsoever. In addition, Contractor represents and warrant that Contractor does not presently perform or intend to perform, during the term of this Agreement, consulting or other services for, or engage in or intend to engage in an employment relationship with, companies and / or individuals who have businesses or proposed businesses in any way involve products or services which would be competitive with the Company’s products or services, or those products or services proposed or in development by the Company during the term of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: V. NO UNFAIR COMPETITION. The Contractor will adhere to ethical business practices during their engagement. The specifics regarding competitive activities will be outlined in a separate policy document.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the detailed non-compete clause with a general statement about 'ethical business practices' and a reference to a 'separate policy document', which is not provided or defined, creates an in-text contradiction. The original agreement includes an explicit non-compete and by creating a 'separate policy document', that replaces the already specified non-compete, creates an uncertainty that contradicts the original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Wherever necessary to carry out the intent of the parties, certain provisions of this Agreement, including, without limitation, Sections I; II; III.A, C, D; IV; V; VI; and VII shall survive the termination of our work for the Company and shall continue in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a detailed non-compete agreement. The changed text replaces this with a general statement about 'ethical business practices' and a reference to a 'separate policy document', which is not defined. This creates an uncertainty, as it is unclear what specific competitive activities are prohibited. The contradicted text states that Section V, which originally contained the non-compete clause, survives termination. However, the changed Section V no longer contains a clear non-compete, creating an ambiguity about what obligations survive, leading to a contradiction. This meets the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' because essential language was removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the provision of paragraph IV(C), this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California. The exclusive jurisdiction for any legal proceeding regarding this Agreement shall be in the courts of said state, and Contractor hereby expressly submits to the jurisdiction of said courts.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the state determined by the Company. The jurisdiction for any legal proceeding regarding this Agreement will be at the company's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies California law as the governing law. The modified text allows the Company to determine the governing law and jurisdiction, which could contradict legal requirements if the contractor is not based in that selected state or if other clauses rely on California law. This is a contradiction with the intent of a mutually agreed upon contract, as the Company would unilaterally decide the legal jurisdiction that the contractor would be binded by.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that any legal action becomes necessary to enforce or interpret the terms of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled, in addition to its court costs, to such reasonable attorney fees, expert witness fees and legal expenses as shall be fixed by a court of competent jurisdiction. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the state determined by the Company. The jurisdiction for any legal proceeding regarding this Agreement will be at the company's discretion. E. Nothing in this Agreement shall limit the remedies available to the Company. Specifically, and without limitation, wherever Contractor has agreed to execute assignment or other documents for the benefit of the Company, Contractor hereby irrevocably appoints the Company as our attorney-in-fact for the limited purpose of executing any and all such documents and performing any and all other acts necessary to give effect and legality to the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies California law as the governing law. The changed text allows the Company to determine the governing law and jurisdiction, which is a direct contradiction. The contradicted text states 'This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the state determined by the Company. The jurisdiction for any legal proceeding regarding this Agreement will be at the company's discretion.' which conflicts with the initial agreement of using California Law. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, where language is altered in one section to contradict obligations in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: F. Contractor understands that the initial term of this Agreement shall be for two (2) years. However, regardless of the term of this Agreement, Contractor understands that the Company may terminate this Agreement for any reason at any time with 10 days prior written notice to Contractor.
perturbation.changed_text: F. The term of this agreement is ongoing, subject to mutual agreement for renewal or termination. The company may terminate this agreement for a cause with 10 days prior written notice to the contractor.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the initial term of the agreement from a fixed two-year period to an 'ongoing' one introduces uncertainty. The Company's ability to terminate the agreement only 'for a cause' with 10 days' notice, now introduces conflict with that idea that the contract is an ongoing, as the termination policy is very limited. Therefore, it is a contradiction and there is now an uncertainty about the actual term length.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor understands that the initial term of this Agreement shall be for two (2) years. However, regardless of the term of this Agreement, Contractor understands that the Company may terminate this Agreement for any reason at any time with 10 days prior written notice to Contractor. II. TRADE SECRETS AND CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION . A. Confidentiality and Confidential Information . Contractor agrees to regard and preserve as confidential all information obtained by Contractor relating or pertaining to (i) the Company’s business, projects, plans, products, planned or proposed products, customers, potential customers, trade secrets, and other confidential information (including business and financial information), and any computer programs and software or unpublished know-how, whether patented or unpatented, and to (ii) all of our activities for or on behalf of the Company, and Contractor agrees not to publish or disclose any part of such information to others or use the same for our own purposes or the purposes of others, during the time Contractor is working for Company or thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the contract had an initial term of two years but could be terminated for any reason with 10 days' notice. The changed text modifies the initial term to 'ongoing' and limits termination to 'for a cause'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states a two-year term and termination 'for any reason'. The change removes the 'for any reason' clause and introduces 'for a cause', which contradicts the original termination terms mentioned in the contradicted text, thereby introducing a strong in-text contradiction.
